title: Secure first confirmed consumer/partner (or proven Zenodo path)
context: The project's Definition of Shipped requires verified acceptance: either a named consumer confirms they will use or accept a release, or a Zenodo release is demonstrably adopted by ≥ 1 external reuser. Until this is achieved, all delivery-dependent tasks remain verifiedNeed:false. This task converts the outreach threads opened in pmc-cancer-outreach-006 into a confirmed commitment. Securing this flips verifiedNeed to true and sets requestor on the relevant tasks.
objective: Convert the M0 outreach into a confirmed first consumer/partner: secure written confirmation from a named lab, advocacy org, or NLP group that they will use or accept a corpus release, OR document a proven Zenodo release path where ≥ 1 external reuser has downloaded or cited the release — and record the evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Either: a named lab/advocacy org/NLP group confirms in writing they will use or accept a corpus release (email or equivalent recorded); the confirmation message and contact are committed as evidence.

Or: a Zenodo release is published, adopted by ≥ 1 external reuser (demonstrable via Zenodo download stats, a citation, a public repo dependency, or a Zenodo derivative), and the evidence is externally verifiable and committed.
The consumption/acceptance mechanism is documented: how the consumer will access, use, or cite the release.
The Steward updates the relevant task JSONs to set verifiedNeed:true and requestor to the named consumer/partner once confirmed.
Any conditions or constraints the partner has placed on the release (e.g., license tier preference, format) are recorded and surfaced to the Maintainer.
resources: pmc-cancer-outreach-006 (outreach threads and Zenodo fallback path)
PLAN.md (Problem & beneficiaries; Success metrics; Definition of Shipped)
output: A committed document recording the confirmed consumer/partner (name, org, contact, confirmation evidence, consumption mechanism) or the Zenodo reuse evidence, with a follow-up note on which task JSONs have been updated to verifiedNeed:true.
